title: B2 Entry Test — Comprehensive Mixed Test
titleTr: B2 Giris Testi — Kapsamli Karma Test
description: This is your B2 ENTRY TEST. Prove you're ready for upper-intermediate English! This comprehensive test covers ALL Module 5 grammar and vocabulary: Past Perfect, Third Conditional, Mixed Conditionals, Gerunds vs Infinitives, Advanced Passive, Collocations, and Idioms. One full professional day — board meeting, investor pitch, team standup, and client negotiation.
descriptionTr: Bu senin B2 GIRIS TESTIN. Ust-orta Ingilizce icin hazir oldugunuzu kanitla! Bu kapsamli test tum Modul 5 dilbilgisi ve kelime bilgisini kapsar: Past Perfect, Ucuncu Kosul, Karisik Kosullar, Gerund vs Infinitive, Ileri Edilgen, Eslesmeler ve Deyimler. Bir tam profesyonel gun — yonetim kurulu toplantisi, yatirimci sunumu, ekip standup'i ve musteri muzakeresi.

vocabulary.example: The team held a retrospective to analyze what went wrong last quarter.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Ekip gecen ceyrek neyin yanlis gittigini analiz etmek icin geriye donuk degerlendirme yapti.
vocabulary.extra: If we had done a retrospective earlier, we would have caught the issue sooner.
vocabulary.extraTr: Daha once retrospektif yapsaydik, sorunu daha erken yakalardik.

vocabulary.example: We always need a contingency plan in case the main strategy fails.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Ana strateji basarisiz olursa diye her zaman bir acil durum planina ihtiyacimiz var.
vocabulary.extra: Having prepared a contingency plan turned out to be a game changer.
vocabulary.extraTr: Acil durum plani hazirlamis olmak oyun degistirici oldu.

vocabulary.example: In hindsight, we should have invested in automation much earlier.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Geriye bakinca, otomasyona cok daha erken yatirim yapmalıydik.
vocabulary.extra: Hindsight is always 20/20 — it's easy to see mistakes after they happen.
vocabulary.extraTr: Geriye bakinca her sey nettir — hatalar olduktan sonra gormek kolaydir.

vocabulary.example: The engineering team achieved a major breakthrough in battery technology.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Muhendislik ekibi batarya teknolojisinde buyuk bir atilim sagladi.
vocabulary.extra: If we hadn't invested in R&D, this breakthrough would never have happened.
vocabulary.extraTr: Ar-Ge'ye yatirim yapmasaydik, bu atilim asla olmazdi.

vocabulary.example: The startup decided to pivot from B2C to B2B after analyzing the market.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Startup pazari analiz ettikten sonra B2C'den B2B'ye yon degistirmeye karar verdi.
vocabulary.extra: Pivoting quickly is essential in the tech industry.
vocabulary.extraTr: Teknoloji sektorunde hizla yon degistirmek cok onemlidir.

vocabulary.example: Building a successful company requires perseverance and patience.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Basarili bir sirket kurmak azim ve sabir gerektirir.
vocabulary.extra: Her perseverance in learning English paid off — she got promoted.
vocabulary.extraTr: Ingilizce ogrenmekteki azmi karsiligini verdi — terfi etti.

vocabulary.example: The product was certified by an international standards organization.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Urun uluslararasi bir standart kurulusu tarafindan belgelendi.
vocabulary.extra: We need to get our process certified before entering the EU market.
vocabulary.extraTr: AB pazarina girmeden once surecimizi sertifikalandirmamiz gerekiyor.

vocabulary.example: Industry benchmarks show that our customer retention rate is above average.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Sektor referans degerleri musteri tutma oranımızın ortalamanin ustunde oldugunu gosteriyor.
vocabulary.extra: If we had benchmarked against competitors earlier, we'd have spotted the gap.
vocabulary.extraTr: Rakiplerle daha once kiyaslama yapsaydik, farki gorurduk.

vocabulary.example: Learning collocations like 'make a decision' instead of 'do a decision' is essential.
vocabulary.exampleTr: 'Do a decision' yerine 'make a decision' gibi birlikte kullanilan kelimeleri ogrenmek cok onemli.
vocabulary.extra: Strong collocations help you sound more natural in English.
vocabulary.extraTr: Guclu eslesmeler Ingilizce'de daha dogal gozukmeni saglar.

vocabulary.example: Knowing idioms like 'get the ball rolling' makes you sound professional.
vocabulary.exampleTr: 'Get the ball rolling' gibi deyimleri bilmek seni profesyonel gosterir.
vocabulary.extra: She suggested learning idioms to improve her fluency.
vocabulary.extraTr: Akiciligini gelistirmek icin deyim ogrenmeyi onerdi.

vocabulary.example: All stakeholders must be consulted before making a major strategic decision.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Buyuk bir stratejik karar vermeden once tum paydaslara danişilmalidir.
vocabulary.extra: The stakeholders were informed that the timeline had been extended.
vocabulary.extraTr: Paydaslar zaman cizelgesinin uzatildigini bilgilendirildi.

vocabulary.example: The merger created incredible synergy between the two teams.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Birlesme iki ekip arasinda inanilmaz bir sinerji yaratti.
vocabulary.extra: We're looking for synergy between marketing and product development.
vocabulary.extraTr: Pazarlama ve urun gelistirme arasinda sinerji ariyoruz.

vocabulary.example: The new fintech company aims to disrupt traditional banking.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Yeni fintech sirketi geleneksel bankaciligi sarsmay hedefliyor.
vocabulary.extra: If they hadn't disrupted the market, the old players would still dominate.
vocabulary.extraTr: Piyasayi sarmasalardi, eski oyuncular hala hakim olurdu.

vocabulary.example: Companies that fail to innovate risk being left behind by competitors.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Yenilik yapamayan sirketler rakiplerin gerisinde kalma riskiyle karsi karsiya.
vocabulary.extra: Innovating requires both creativity and the willingness to take risks.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yenilik yapmak hem yaraticilik hem de risk alma istekliligi gerektirir.

vocabulary.example: We need to build a sustainable business model, not just chase short-term profits.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Sadece kisa vadeli karlar pesinde kosmak yerine surdurulebilir bir is modeli kurmamız gerekiyor.
vocabulary.extra: Sustainable growth is preferred over rapid but unstable expansion.
vocabulary.extraTr: Surdurulebilir buyume, hizli ama istikrarsiz genislemeden tercih edilir.

vocabulary.example: Investors want to see that your solution is scalable across multiple markets.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Yatirimcilar cozumunuzun birden fazla pazarda olceklenebilir oldugunu gormek istiyor.
vocabulary.extra: The platform was designed to be scalable from day one.
vocabulary.extraTr: Platform ilk gunden olceklenebilir olarak tasarlandi.

vocabulary.example: Our supply chain proved to be resilient during the global crisis.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Tedarik zincirimiz kuresel kriz sirasinda dayanikli oldugunu kaniladi.
vocabulary.extra: A resilient team doesn't give up when challenges arise.
vocabulary.extraTr: Dayanikli bir ekip zorluklar ciktiginda pes etmez.

vocabulary.example: Agile methodology helps teams adapt quickly to changing requirements.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Cevik metodoloji ekiplerin degisen gereksinimlere hizla uyum saglamasina yardimci olur.
vocabulary.extra: Being agile means embracing change rather than resisting it.
vocabulary.extraTr: Cevik olmak degisime direnmek yerine kucaklamak demektir.

vocabulary.example: We make data-driven decisions — no more relying on gut feelings.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Veriye dayali kararlar aliyoruz — artik ice dogusuna guvenme yok.
vocabulary.extra: A data-driven approach helped us identify the pain points faster.
vocabulary.extraTr: Veriye dayali bir yaklasim sorunlu noktalari daha hizli tespite yardimci oldu.

vocabulary.example: The project requires a cross-functional team from engineering, marketing, and sales.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Proje muhendislik, pazarlama ve satisindan olusan departmanlar arasi bir ekip gerektiriyor.
vocabulary.extra: Cross-functional collaboration is key to successful product launches.
vocabulary.extraTr: Departmanlar arasi is birligi basarili urun lansmanlarinin anahtaridir.

vocabulary.example: Our startup is part of a growing tech ecosystem in Istanbul.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Startup'imiz Istanbul'da buyuyen bir teknoloji ekosisteminin parcasi.
vocabulary.extra: Building a strong ecosystem around your product increases customer loyalty.
vocabulary.extraTr: Urunun etrafinda guclu bir ekosistem kurmak musteri sadakatini artirir.

vocabulary.example: The client expects all deliverables to be completed by end of month.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Musteri tum teslim edilecek islerin ay sonuna kadar tamamlanmasini bekliyor.
vocabulary.extra: We need to define the deliverables clearly before starting the project.
vocabulary.extraTr: Projeye baslamadan once ciktilari net olarak tanimlamamiz gerekiyor.

vocabulary.example: I don't have the bandwidth to take on another project right now.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Su anda baska bir projeyi ustlenecek kapasitem yok.
vocabulary.extra: Before committing, check if the team has enough bandwidth.
vocabulary.extraTr: Taahhut etmeden once ekibin yeterli kapasitesi olup olmadigini kontrol et.

vocabulary.example: We should leverage our existing partnerships to expand into Asia.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Asya'ya acilmak icin mevcut ortakliklarimizi kullanmaliyiz.
vocabulary.extra: Leveraging data analytics gave us a competitive edge.
vocabulary.extraTr: Veri analitigini kullanmak bize rekabet avantaji sagladi.

vocabulary.turkish: en uygun hale getirmek / optimize etmek
vocabulary.example: We optimized the supply chain to reduce delivery times by 30%.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Teslimat surelerini %30 azaltmak icin tedarik zincirini optimize ettik.
vocabulary.extra: The landing page was optimized after analyzing user behavior data.
vocabulary.extraTr: Acilis sayfasi kullanici davranis verileri analiz edildikten sonra optimize edildi.

vocabulary.example: We iterate on the product design based on user feedback every sprint.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Her sprintte kullanici geri bildirimlerine dayanarak urun tasarimini gelistiriyoruz.
vocabulary.extra: If we hadn't iterated quickly, the product would have failed at launch.
vocabulary.extraTr: Hizla yinelemeseydik, urun lansinda basarisiz olurdu.

vocabulary.example: A strong feedback loop between customers and developers improves product quality.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Musteriler ve gelistiriciler arasindaki guclu bir geri bildirim dongusu urun kalitesini arttirir.
vocabulary.extra: We established a feedback loop to ensure continuous improvement.
vocabulary.extraTr: Surekli iyilestirmeyi saglamak icin bir geri bildirim dongusu olusturduk.

vocabulary.example: The biggest pain point for our customers is the slow checkout process.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Musterilerimiz icin en buyuk sorunlu nokta yavas odeme sureci.
vocabulary.extra: Identifying pain points is the first step to building a great product.
vocabulary.extraTr: Sorunlu noktalari belirlemek harika bir urun yapmanin ilk adimidir.

vocabulary.example: Our value proposition is simple: faster delivery at lower cost.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Deger onerimiz basit: daha dusuk maliyetle daha hizli teslimat.
vocabulary.extra: A clear value proposition differentiates you from competitors.
vocabulary.extraTr: Net bir deger onerisi sizi rakiplerden ayirir.

vocabulary.example: The go-to-market strategy for the new product was launched last week.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Yeni urun icin pazara giris stratejisi gecen hafta hayata gecirildi.
vocabulary.extra: A solid go-to-market plan is critical for any product launch.
vocabulary.extraTr: Saglam bir pazara giris plani her urun lansmani icin kritiktir.

grammar.title: Full B1→B2 Grammar Review
grammar.explanation: This is your comprehensive grammar review covering everything from Module 5. You need to master Past Perfect, Third Conditional, Mixed Conditionals, Gerunds vs Infinitives, Advanced Passive Voice, Collocations, and Idioms. If you can use all of these correctly, you're ready for B2!
grammar.explanationTr: Bu Modul 5'ten her seyi kapsayan kapsamli dilbilgisi tekrarin. Past Perfect, Ucuncu Kosul, Karisik Kosullar, Gerund vs Infinitive, Ileri Edilgen Cati, Eslesmeler ve Deyimlerde ustalasman gerekiyor. Bunlarin hepsini dogru kullanabilirsen, B2'ye hazirsin!
table: Past Perfect: had + past participle
By the time we launched, the team had already tested every feature.
The investor had reviewed our pitch deck before the meeting started.
Use when one past event happened BEFORE another past event.
Third Conditional: If + had + pp, would have + pp
If we had pivoted earlier, we would have saved six months of development.
If the team hadn't iterated quickly, the product would have failed.
Talks about unreal past — things that DIDN'T happen but could have.
Mixed Conditionals: past condition → present result
If we had invested in AI last year, we would be the market leader now.
If she hadn't studied English, she wouldn't have this job today.
Mix past cause with present/future effect — very advanced!
Gerunds vs Infinitives: -ing form vs to + base verb
We recommend investing in sustainable solutions. / We decided to pivot.
Avoid cutting corners. / We managed to optimize the process.
Some verbs take gerund (enjoy, avoid, suggest), others take infinitive (decide, manage, plan).
Advanced Passive: complex passive structures
The deliverables are expected to be completed by Friday.
The product is reported to have been certified by ISO.
Passive + infinitive and passive + perfect infinitive — formal & professional.
Collocations: natural word partnerships
Make a decision (NOT do a decision). / Take responsibility (NOT get responsibility).
Strong collocations: conduct research, draw a conclusion, reach an agreement.
Memorize these as fixed pairs — they make your English sound native.
Idioms: fixed expressions with non-literal meaning
Let's get the ball rolling. / We need to bite the bullet.
At the end of the day, the big picture matters. / It was a piece of cake.
Use in meetings and casual office talk — avoid in formal legal/technical writing.

tips.en: Third Conditional trick: 'If + HAD + past participle, WOULD HAVE + past participle.' Both clauses reference the past. 'If we had known, we would have acted differently.'
tips.tr: Ucuncu Kosul hilesi: 'If + HAD + pp, WOULD HAVE + pp.' Her iki cumle de gecmise referans verir. 'Bilseydik, farkli davranirdik.'

tips.en: Mixed Conditional: past → present. 'If I had studied medicine (past), I would be a doctor now (present).' The time in each clause is DIFFERENT.
tips.tr: Karisik Kosul: gecmis → simdiki. 'Tip okusaydim (gecmis), simdi doktor olurdum (simdi).' Her cumledeki zaman FARKLI.

tips.en: Gerund after prepositions: 'interested in learning,' 'good at negotiating,' 'tired of waiting.' ALWAYS use -ing after a preposition.
tips.tr: Edatlardan sonra gerund: 'interested in learning,' 'good at negotiating,' 'tired of waiting.' Edattan sonra HER ZAMAN -ing kullan.

tips.en: Advanced Passive: 'is expected to be' / 'is reported to have been.' Use these in presentations and reports for a professional tone.
tips.tr: Ileri Edilgen: 'is expected to be' / 'is reported to have been.' Profesyonel ton icin sunumlarda ve raporlarda kullan.

tips.en: Collocation test: if it 'sounds right' to a native speaker, it's probably a collocation. 'Make a mistake' sounds right; 'do a mistake' sounds wrong.
tips.tr: Esleme testi: anadil konusmacisina 'dogru geliyorsa' muhtemelen bir eslesmedir. 'Make a mistake' dogru gelir; 'do a mistake' yanlis gelir.

tips.en: Combine everything in one sentence: 'If we had leveraged our data-driven approach earlier, we would have been able to get the ball rolling on the pivot much sooner.'
tips.tr: Her seyi tek cumlede birlestir: 'Veriye dayali yaklasimimizi daha once kullansaydik, yon degisikligini cok daha erken baslatabilirdik.'

reading.text:
LESSONS FROM BUILDING A LOGISTICS TECH STARTUP

Three years ago, I had an idea: what if we could disrupt the traditional logistics industry with a data-driven platform? In hindsight, I had no idea how challenging the journey would be — but also how rewarding.

The first year was brutal. We had built a prototype, but it wasn't scalable. Our go-to-market strategy had been designed for B2C, but after conducting extensive research, we realized the real pain point was in B2B logistics. We had to pivot — completely. If we hadn't made that decision, the company would have failed within six months.

The pivot required perseverance. Our cross-functional team — engineers, designers, and sales reps — worked around the clock. We iterated on the product every two weeks, establishing a feedback loop with our first three enterprise clients. Each iteration was optimized based on real usage data, not assumptions.

By year two, we had achieved a major breakthrough. Our platform had been certified by two international standards organizations, and three major logistics companies had agreed to pilot our solution. The value proposition was clear: 40% faster route optimization and 25% cost reduction. Investors started paying attention.

The board meeting where we secured Series A funding was a game changer. We had prepared a comprehensive pitch, leveraging every data point we had collected. The stakeholders were impressed by our resilient team culture and our agile methodology. One investor said, 'If you hadn't shown this level of innovation, we wouldn't be having this conversation.'

Now, in year three, we're scaling across three markets. Our ecosystem includes 50+ integration partners, and our deliverables consistently exceed benchmarks. The team has grown from 4 to 45 people, and every new hire is expected to hit the ground running.

Looking back, the biggest lesson is this: sustainable growth beats rapid expansion. We could have cut corners to grow faster, but we chose to optimize for quality. At the end of the day, building something that lasts requires both innovation and discipline.

If someone had told me three years ago that we would be where we are today, I would have taken it with a grain of salt. But here we are — proof that perseverance, data-driven decisions, and a willingness to pivot can turn an idea into reality.
reading.textTr:
BIR LOJISTIK TEKNOLOJI STARTUP'I KURMAKTAN DERSLER

Uc yil once bir fikrim vardi: ya geleneksel lojistik sektorunu veriye dayali bir platformla sarssak? Geriye bakinca, yolculugun ne kadar zor olacagi hakkinda hicbir fikrim yoktu — ama ayni zamanda ne kadar odullendirici.

Ilk yil acımasızdi. Bir prototip yapmistik ama olceklenebilir degildi. Pazara giris stratejimiz B2C icin tasarlanmisti ama kapsamli arastirma yaptiktan sonra gercek sorunlu noktanin B2B lojistikte oldugunu fark ettik. Yon degistirmemiz gerekiyordu — tamamen. O karari vermeseydik, sirket alti ay icinde basarisiz olurdu.

Yon degisikligi azim gerektirdi. Departmanlar arasi ekibimiz — muhendisler, tasarimcilar ve satis temsilcileri — gece gunduz calisti. Urunu her iki haftada bir yineledik ve ilk uc kurumsal musterimizle geri bildirim dongusu olusturduk. Her yineleme varsayimlara degil gercek kullanim verilerine dayali olarak optimize edildi.

Ikinci yilda buyuk bir atilim sagladik. Platformumuz iki uluslararasi standart kurulusu tarafindan sertifikalandirildi ve uc buyuk lojistik sirketi cozumumuzu pilot olarak denemeyi kabul etti. Deger onerisi netti: %40 daha hizli rota optimizasyonu ve %25 maliyet dusurme. Yatirimcilar dikkat etmeye basladi.

Seri A fonlamasini guvence altına aldigimiz yonetim kurulu toplantisi bir oyun degistiriciydi. Topledigimiz her veri noktasini kullanarak kapsamli bir sunum hazirlamistik. Paydaslar dayanikli ekip kulturumuz ve cevik metodolojimizden etkilendi. Bir yatirimci dedi ki, 'Bu seviyede inovasyon gostermeseydiniz, bu konusmayi yapmiyor olurduk.'

Simdi ucuncu yilda uc pazarda buyuyoruz. Ekosistemimiz 50'den fazla entegrasyon partneri icerir ve ciktilarimiz surekli referans degerleri asiyor. Ekip 4 kisiden 45 kisiye buyudu ve her yeni ise alinanin hemen ise koyulmasi bekleniyor.

Geriye bakinca en buyuk ders su: surdurulebilir buyume hizli genislemeden ustundur. Daha hizli buyumek icin kaliteden kisabilirdik ama kalite icin optimize etmeyi sectik. Gunun sonunda kalici bir sey insa etmek hem inovasyon hem de disiplin gerektirir.

Uc yil once biri bana bugun bulundugumuz yerde olacagimizi soylese, ihtiyatla karsilardim. Ama buradayiz — azimin, veriye dayali kararlarin ve yon degistirme istekliliginin bir fikri gercege donusturebileceginin kaniti.

questions.question: Why did the startup need to pivot in the first year?
questions.questionTr: Startup neden ilk yilda yon degistirmek zorunda kaldi?
options: The real pain point was in B2B, not B2C

questions.question: What grammar structure is used in: 'If we hadn't made that decision, the company would have failed'?
questions.questionTr: 'O karari vermeseydik, sirket basarisiz olurdu' cumlesinde hangi yapi kullanilmis?

questions.question: What was the startup's value proposition?
options: 40% faster route optimization and 25% cost reduction

questions.question: What did the investor say during the Series A meeting?
questions.questionTr: Yatirimci Seri A toplantisinda ne dedi?
options: He said the team was too small
If they hadn't shown innovation, the conversation wouldn't be happening
He wanted to see more revenue

questions.question: According to the text, what is the biggest lesson from building the startup?
questions.questionTr: Metne gore startup kurmaktan alinan en buyuk ders nedir?
options: Hire as many people as possible

dialog.title: Eyup's Full Professional Day — B2 Challenge
dialog.titleTr: Eyup'un Tam Profesyonel Gunu — B2 Meydan Okumasi
dialog.context: Eyup'un B2 seviyesindeki zorlu bir is gunu: sabah yonetim kurulu toplantisi (CEO Ayse), ogle yatirimci sunumu (Mr. Chen), ogleden sonra ekip standup'i (Selin, ekip lideri), ve aksam musteri muzakeresi (Sarah, Londra'dan).

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Good morning, everyone. Before we get started, I want to address the elephant in the room — last quarter's revenue was 15% below our benchmark. Eyup, you've been leading the retrospective. What did you find?
lines.textTr: Gunaydin millet. Baslamadan once odadaki fili ele almak istiyorum — gecen ceyregin geliri referans degerimizin %15 altindaydi. Eyup, retrospektifi sen yonetiyordun. Ne buldun?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thank you, Ayse. In hindsight, there were three key issues. First, we had relied too heavily on a single client for 40% of our revenue. Second, our go-to-market strategy hadn't been optimized for the new market segment. And third, we dropped the ball on the Q3 deliverables.
lines.textTr: Tesekkurler, Ayse. Geriye bakinca uc temel sorun vardi. Birincisi, gelirimizin %40'i icin tek bir musteriye cok fazla guvenmistik. Ikincisi, pazara giris stratejimiz yeni pazar segmenti icin optimize edilmemisti. Ucuncusu, 3. ceyrek ciktilarinda hata yaptik.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Honest assessment. If we had diversified our client base earlier, we wouldn't be in this position now. What's the contingency plan?
lines.textTr: Durüst degerlendirme. Musteri tabanımızı daha erken cesitlendirsseydik, su anda bu durumda olmazdik. Acil durum plani ne?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: We've already started pivoting. The cross-functional team has been working on a data-driven approach to identify new pain points in the B2B logistics space. We have three potential enterprise clients in the pipeline, and our value proposition has been refined based on the feedback loop we established.
lines.textTr: Coktan yon degistirmeye basladik. Departmanlar arasi ekip B2B lojistik alaninda yeni sorunlu noktalari belirlemek icin veriye dayali bir yaklasim uzerinde calisiyor. Is hattinda uc potansiyel kurumsal musterimiz var ve deger onerimiz olusturdugumuz geri bildirim dongusune dayanarak rafine edildi.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Good. The board expects to see a breakthrough by Q2. Let's not cut corners this time — I want sustainable, scalable growth. The bottom line is: we need 25% revenue increase by June.
lines.textTr: Iyi. Yonetim kurulu 2. ceyrekte bir atilim gormeyi bekliyor. Bu sefer kaliteden kismamayelim — surdurulebilir, olceklenebilir buyume istiyorum. Sonuc olarak: Hazirana kadar %25 gelir artisi gerekiyor.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Understood. If we leverage our existing ecosystem and optimize the sales funnel, I'm confident we can hit that target. I'll keep the board in the loop with weekly reports.
lines.textTr: Anlasildi. Mevcut ekosistemimizi kullanir ve satis hunisini optimize edersek, o hedefe ulasabilecegimizden eminim. Yonetim kurulunu haftalik raporlarla bilgilendirmeye devam edecegim.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Eyup, thank you for the pitch. Your data-driven approach is impressive. However, I need to understand — if you had had more funding last year, what would you have done differently?
lines.textTr: Eyup, sunum icin tesekkurler. Veriye dayali yaklsiminiz etkileyici. Ancak anlamamız gerekiyor — gecen yıl daha fazla fonlamanzı olsaydı, ne farkli yapardınız?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Great question, Mr. Chen. If we had secured funding six months earlier, we would have been able to hire a dedicated R&D team. That would have accelerated our product iteration cycle from four weeks to two. In hindsight, the delayed funding forced us to be more agile and resilient, which actually became our competitive advantage.
lines.textTr: Harika soru, Mr. Chen. Fonlamayi alti ay once guvence altina alsaydik, ozel bir Ar-Ge ekibi ise alabilirdik. Bu urun yineleme dongumuzu dort haftadan ikiye hizlandirirdi. Geriye bakinca, geciken fonlama bizi daha cevik ve dayanikli olmaya zorladi, bu da aslinda rekabet avantajimiz oldu.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Interesting. And what about your value proposition? How does it differentiate you from the established players who are also trying to disrupt logistics?
lines.textTr: Ilginc. Peki deger oneriniz? Sizi lojistigi sarsmaya calisan koklesmis oyunculardan nasil ayiriyor?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Two things, Mr. Chen. First, our platform has been certified by ISO and TUV, which established players haven't achieved yet. Second, we avoided reinventing the wheel — instead, we focused on optimizing existing processes. Our clients report a 40% improvement in delivery times. The benchmarks speak for themselves.
lines.textTr: Iki sey, Mr. Chen. Birincisi, platformumuz koklesmis oyuncularin henuz basaramadigi ISO ve TUV tarafindan sertifikalandirildi. Ikincisi, tekerlergi yeniden icat etmekten kacindik — bunun yerine mevcut surecleri optimize etmeye odaklandik. Musterilerimiz teslimat surelerinde %40 iyilesme bildiriyor. Referans degerleri kendileri icin konusuyor.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: If you hadn't shown these numbers, I wouldn't have believed the traction. I'm interested in exploring a Series B investment. Let's touch base next week with your CFO.
lines.textTr: Bu rakamlari gostermeseydiniz, cekis gucune inanmazdim. Seri B yatirimini kesfetmekle ilgileniyorum. Gelecek hafta CFO'nuzla goruselim.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: That would be fantastic, Mr. Chen. I'll get the ball rolling on the due diligence documents and have everything ready for your review. Thank you for your time.
lines.textTr: Muhtesem olur, Mr. Chen. Durum tespiti belgeleri uzerinde isi baslatacagim ve incelemeniz icin her seyi hazirlayacagim. Zamaniniz icin tesekkurler.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Alright team, standup time. Quick updates — where are we on the sprint deliverables? Eyup, you go first.
lines.textTr: Pekala ekip, standup zamani. Hizli guncellemeler — sprint ciktilarinda neredeyiz? Eyup, sen once.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Morning, Selin. I managed to finalize the integration module yesterday. The learning curve was steep, but the team adapted quickly. We also established a new feedback loop with the QA team — they identified two critical bugs that had been overlooked.
lines.textTr: Gunaydin, Selin. Dun entegrasyon modulunu tamamlamayi basardim. Ogrenme sureci dikti ama ekip hizla uyum sagladi. Ayrica QA ekibiyle yeni bir geri bildirim dongusu olusturduk — gozden kacirilan iki kritik hata tespit ettiler.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Good catch on those bugs. If they hadn't been found before release, we would have had a serious problem. What about the API optimization?
lines.textTr: Bu hatalari yakalamak iyi oldu. Yayindan once bulunamasalardi, ciddi bir sorunumuz olurdu. API optimizasyonu ne durumda?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: It's in progress. We decided to iterate one more time based on the latest performance data. The current version is expected to be ready by Thursday. I also recommend investing in automated testing — it would save us 10 hours per sprint.
lines.textTr: Devam ediyor. En son performans verilerine dayanarak bir kez daha yinelemeye karar verdik. Mevcut surumun Persembe'ye kadar hazir olmasi bekleniyor. Ayrica otomatik teste yatirim yapmayi oneririm — sprint basina 10 saat tasarruf saglar.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Agreed. Let's raise the bar on test coverage. Make sure the new hires are up to speed on our coding standards. I don't want anyone cutting corners.
lines.textTr: Katiliyorum. Test kapsami konusunda standardi yukseltelim. Yeni calisanlarin kodlama standartlarimiz hakkinda bilgi seviyesinde oldugundan emin ol. Kimsenin kaliteden kismasini istemiyorum.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Already on it. I've been mentoring them this week — they're eager to hit the ground running. I'll have them shadow me during the client call this afternoon.
lines.textTr: Zaten uzerindeyim. Bu hafta onlara mentorluk yapiyorum — hemen ise koyulmak icin istekliler. Ogleden sonraki musteri aramasinda beni izlemelerini saglayacagim.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Eyup, thanks for making time. I'll cut to the chase — we've been evaluating three vendors, and your platform is the strongest technically. But I need to understand the pricing before making a decision.
lines.textTr: Eyup, zaman ayirdigin icin tesekkurler. Sadede gelecegim — uc saticiy degerlendirdik ve platformunuz teknik olarak en guclü. Ama karar vermeden once fiyatlandirmayi anlamamız gerekiyor.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Of course, Sarah. I appreciate your directness. Before we discuss pricing, I'd like to make sure we see eye to eye on the scope. Your team mentioned needing cross-functional integration with your existing ERP system, correct?
lines.textTr: Tabii ki, Sarah. Aciksorulugunuzu takdir ediyorum. Fiyatlandirmayi tartismadan once, kapsam konusunda hemfikir oldugumuuzdan emin olmak istiyorum. Ekibiniz mevcut ERP sisteminizle departmanlar arasi entegrasyon gerektigini belirtti, dogru mu?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: That's right. And we need the solution to be scalable — we're planning to expand into five new European markets next year. If the system couldn't handle that growth, it would be a dealbreaker.
lines.textTr: Dogru. Ve cozumun olceklenebilir olmasina ihtiyacimiz var — gelecek yil bes yeni Avrupa pazarina genislemeyi planliyoruz. Sistem bu buyumeyi kaldiramzsaa, anlasmayi bozar.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Absolutely. Our platform was designed to be scalable from day one. We've already been certified for EU data compliance, and our ecosystem supports integration with all major ERP providers. To give you a ballpark figure, we're looking at $180,000 annually for the enterprise package, which includes full support and quarterly optimization reviews.
lines.textTr: Kesinlikle. Platformumuz ilk gunden olceklenebilir olarak tasarlandi. AB veri uyumlulugu icin zaten sertifikalandirildik ve ekosistemimiz tum buyuk ERP saglayicilariyla entegrasyonu destekliyor. Size yaklasik bir rakam vermek gerekirse, kurumsal paket icin yillik 180.000 dolar goruyoruz, bu tam destek ve ücaylik optimizasyon incelemelerini iceriyor.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: That's within our budget range. But if we committed to a three-year contract, would you be able to offer a better rate? We'd also want a contingency clause in case the deliverables aren't met.
lines.textTr: Bu butce araligimizda. Ama uc yillik bir sozlesme taahhut edersek, daha iyi bir fiyat sunabilir misiniz? Ayrica ciktilar karsilanmadiginda bir acil durum maddesi de istiyoruz.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: For a three-year commitment, we could offer a 15% discount — that brings it down to $153,000 per year. And yes, we're happy to include a contingency clause. We stand behind our deliverables. This would be a win-win for both of us — you get long-term stability, and we get a committed partnership.
lines.textTr: Uc yillik taahhut icin %15 indirim sunabiliriz — bu yillik 153.000 dolara duser. Ve evet, bir acil durum maddesi eklememizden memnuniyet duyariz. Ciktilarimizin arkasinda dururuz. Bu her ikimiz icin de kazanc olur — siz uzun vadeli istikrar elde edersiniz, biz de taahhutlu bir ortaklik.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I like the sound of that. If you had approached us a year ago, we wouldn't have been ready — but the timing is perfect now. I'll discuss this with my board and get back to you by Friday. Please keep me in the loop on any product updates in the meantime.
lines.textTr: Kulaga hos geliyor. Bir yil once bize yaklassaydiniz, hazir olmazdik — ama zamanlama simdi mukemmel. Bunu yonetim kurulumla tartisip Cumaya kadar size donecegim. Bu arada herhangi bir urun guncellemesi hakkinda beni bilgilendirmeye devam edin.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Absolutely, Sarah. I'll send over the detailed proposal with all the benchmarks and the contingency terms by end of day. It was a pleasure speaking with you — I'm confident this partnership will be a game changer for both of us.
lines.textTr: Kesinlikle, Sarah. Tum referans degerleri ve acil durum sartlariyla detayli teklifi gun sonuna kadar gonderecegim. Sizinle konusmak bir zevkti — bu ortakligin ikimiz icin de oyun degistirici olacagindan eminim.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: What a day. This morning, the board meeting was intense — Ayse pushed hard on the revenue gap, but we had a solid contingency plan ready. Then the investor pitch with Mr. Chen went better than expected — he's interested in Series B. The standup with Selin was productive, and the bugs that had been overlooked were caught just in time. And the negotiation with Sarah? If someone had told me two years ago that I'd be negotiating a $450,000 enterprise deal in English, I would have laughed. But perseverance pays off. B2, here I come.
lines.textTr: Ne gunmus. Bu sabah yonetim kurulu toplantisi yogundu — Ayse gelir acigini zorladi ama saglam bir acil durum planimiz hazirdi. Sonra Mr. Chen ile yatirimci sunumu beklenenden iyi gitti — Seri B ile ilgileniyor. Selin ile standup verimliydi ve gozden kacirilan hatalar tam zamaninda yakalandi. Sarah ile muzakere? Iki yil once biri bana Ingilizce 450.000 dolarlik bir kurumsal anlasma muzakere edecegimi soylese, gulerdim. Ama azim karsiligini verir. B2, geliyorum.

questions.question: What was the main issue discussed at the board meeting?
questions.questionTr: Yonetim kurulu toplantisinda tartisilan ana sorun neydi?

questions.question: What grammar structure is used in: 'If we had diversified earlier, we wouldn't be in this position now'?
questions.questionTr: 'Daha once cesitlendirseydik, simdi bu durumda olmazdik' cumlesinde hangi yapi kullanilmis?

questions.question: What convinced Mr. Chen to explore Series B investment?
questions.questionTr: Mr. Chen'i Seri B yatirimini kesfetmeye ne ikna etti?
options: The data, certifications, and 40% delivery improvement

questions.question: What annual price did Eyup offer Sarah for a three-year commitment?
questions.questionTr: Eyup Sarah'a uc yillik taahhut icin ne kadar yillik fiyat sundu?

questions.question: How many different professional scenarios does Eyup handle in this day?
questions.questionTr: Eyup bu gunde kac farkli profesyonel senaryo yonetiyor?
options: 4 — board meeting, investor pitch, standup, client negotiation

fillBlanks.sentence: If we ___ invested in automation earlier, we would have saved thousands.
By the time the meeting started, the team ___ already prepared the presentation.
If she had studied abroad, she ___ be fluent in English now.
fillBlanks.hint: Mixed Conditional — past cause, present result

fillBlanks.sentence: We recommend ___ in sustainable solutions for long-term growth.
The deliverables are expected ___ be completed by Friday.
You should make a decision, not ___ a decision.
If we hadn't ___ quickly, the product would have failed at launch.
The startup decided ___ pivot from B2C to B2B.
The platform ___ been certified by two international organizations.
If the team had had more ___, they would have finished earlier.
Our ___ proposition is faster delivery at lower cost.
The CEO avoided ___ corners on product safety.
In ___, we should have diversified our client base earlier.
The company managed ___ achieve a major breakthrough.
A strong feedback ___ improves product quality continuously.

multipleChoice.question: 'If we had pivoted earlier, we would have saved six months.' Bu hangi yapi?
'If she had studied medicine, she would be a doctor now.' Bu hangi yapi?
'The product is reported to have been certified.' Bu hangi yapi?
'If we had known, we would have acted differently.' Hangi zaman diliminden bahsediliyor?
Hangisi dogru Past Perfect cumlesi?
options: I had finished the report before the meeting.
I will have finished the report.

multipleChoice.question: Hangi cumlede Mixed Conditional var?
options: If I study, I will pass.
If I studied, I would pass.
If I had studied, I would have passed.
If I had studied harder, I would be a manager now.

sentenceOrder.correct: If we had pivoted earlier we would have saved time
The deliverables are expected to be completed by Friday
By the time the meeting started we had already prepared
If she had studied abroad she would be fluent now
We recommend investing in sustainable and scalable solutions
The platform has been certified by an international organization
Our value proposition is faster delivery at lower cost
A strong feedback loop improves product quality continuously
The cross-functional team managed to achieve a breakthrough
In hindsight we should have diversified our client base

translation.turkish: Daha once yon degistirseydik, alti ay tasarruf ederdik.
translation.english: If we had pivoted earlier, we would have saved six months.
acceptAlso: If we had pivoted sooner, we would have saved six months

translation.turkish: Toplanti basladiginda ekip sunumu coktan hazirlamisti.
translation.english: By the time the meeting started, the team had already prepared the presentation.
acceptAlso: The team had already prepared the presentation by the time the meeting started

translation.turkish: Yurt disinda okusaydi, simdi akici Ingilizce konusurdu.
translation.english: If she had studied abroad, she would be fluent in English now.
acceptAlso: If she had studied abroad, she would speak fluent English now

translation.english: We recommend investing in sustainable solutions.
acceptAlso: We suggest investing in sustainable solutions

translation.english: The deliverables are expected to be completed by Friday.
acceptAlso: The deliverables are expected to be finished by Friday

translation.turkish: Geriye bakinca, musteri tabanımızı daha erken cesitlendirmeliydik.
translation.english: In hindsight, we should have diversified our client base earlier.
acceptAlso: Looking back, we should have diversified our client base earlier

translation.turkish: Departmanlar arasi ekip buyuk bir atilim saglamayi basardi.
translation.english: The cross-functional team managed to achieve a major breakthrough.
acceptAlso: The cross-functional team achieved a major breakthrough

translation.turkish: O rakamlari gostermeseydiniz, cekis gucune inanmazdim.
translation.english: If you hadn't shown those numbers, I wouldn't have believed the traction.
acceptAlso: If you had not shown those numbers, I would not have believed the traction

translation.turkish: Deger onerimiz basit: daha dusuk maliyetle daha hizli teslimat.
translation.english: Our value proposition is simple: faster delivery at lower cost.
acceptAlso: Our value proposition is straightforward: faster delivery at a lower cost

translation.turkish: Piyasayi sarmasalardi, eski oyuncular hala hakim olurdu.
translation.english: If they hadn't disrupted the market, the old players would still dominate.
acceptAlso: If they had not disrupted the market, the old players would still be dominant
